behaviors.description: Required patient fields (name, date of birth, insurance ID) are validated before any downstream processing and missing fields produce a clear error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: intake orchestrator must gate downstream calls on validator output and aggregate all missing fields. Unit tests of validator logic alone miss the 'before any downstream processing' contract. Higher levels rejected: no real OS/network/subprocess or AI-output dependence is required by this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Wire validator + intake orchestrator + downstream service fakes; submit payload missing multiple required fields; assert error lists all missing fields and assert insurance/triage/scheduling modules are not invoked.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, dates more than 150 years ago, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: This is pure validation logic on one input field with deterministic rules; real infra and cross-module wiring are not required to expose failures.
behaviors.test_description: Test DOB validator function with boundary and invalid-format cases: future date, exactly 150-year boundary, older than 150 years, and malformed strings.

behaviors.description: The insurance eligibility check sends the correct patient ID and plan code to the payer API and correctly interprets `eligible` vs `not eligible` vs `pending`.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets external payer API request/response boundary. Real HTTP contract issues (serialization, status/body handling) can be hidden by in-process mocks. Integration rejected because behavior text says 'to the payer API', indicating real protocol boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a real HTTP test endpoint that records requests and returns each eligibility variant; assert outbound payload fields (patient ID, plan code) and mapped internal state for eligible/not eligible/pending.

behaviors.description: When the payer API is unreachable, the system marks eligibility as `pending` and continues the intake rather than blocking.
behaviors.justification: Unreachability is an infrastructure failure mode (connection refused/timeout/network error). Mocked clients can miss real transport behavior and retry/timeouts. Higher levels rejected: no AI-output quality check or multi-agentic sequencing required by this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Force real HTTP unreachable conditions (e.g., closed port/timeout endpoint); execute intake; assert eligibility becomes pending and pipeline proceeds to later steps instead of aborting.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation includes the reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric in the model input payload.
behaviors.justification: This checks wiring between intake data, config, and LLM client payload construction. It does not require model judgment correctness. Agentic rejected because behavior is exclusively about request payload contents, not output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run triage service with stub model client capturing request payload; assert symptoms and emergency rubric are both present and correctly mapped.

behaviors.description: The live AI symptom triage assigns high urgency to symptom combinations that indicate emergency conditions (for example chest pain plus shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model clinical reasoning output; deterministic stubs always pass and cannot detect misclassification. Workflow rejected: this behavior is a single model decision, not multiple agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Call the live triage model repeatedly with emergency symptom combinations and assert urgency classification meets high-urgency expectation threshold.

behaviors.description: The triage result parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field (not only a numeric score).
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at parser-to-persistence handoff: explanation could be parsed but dropped when stored. Unit parser tests alone can miss storage mapping defects. Higher levels rejected: no real AI judgment or external infra required by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Feed deterministic triage response containing score + explanation through parser and repository layer; assert persisted record includes non-empty clinician-readable explanation field.

behaviors.description: The live AI symptom triage explanation cites case-specific symptoms and urgency rationale, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: This is output-quality evaluation of live model explanations; mocks cannot reveal generic vs case-specific reasoning failures. Workflow rejected because behavior evaluates one agentic output, not a multi-step agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run live triage on varied symptom cases; assert explanations mention case-specific symptoms and explicit urgency rationale rather than generic templates.

behaviors.description: An appointment is scheduled in the first available slot that matches the assigned urgency level: high urgency gets same-day, medium gets within 48 hours.
behaviors.justification: Needs scheduling logic + calendar DB state interaction; pure unit tests can miss slot-query/order bugs across modules. System rejected: can be caught with local DB/module wiring without real external infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Seed local calendar DB with mixed availability; run scheduler for high and medium urgency inputs; assert chosen slot is first valid one and respects same-day/48-hour policy.

behaviors.description: The scheduling step does not double-book a time slot even when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is concurrent interaction between scheduling service and shared calendar DB transaction/locking behavior. Unit tests of slot selection miss race conditions. System considered but rejected because behavior can be reproduced with local concurrent workers and real local DB semantics (no external OS/network API requirement beyond integration scope).
behaviors.test_description: Execute two concurrent intake scheduling attempts targeting the same slot against a real local calendar DB with production transaction settings; assert only one booking succeeds for that slot.

behaviors.description: The confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and preparation instructions appropriate to visit type.
behaviors.justification: Primarily deterministic message composition/template logic from known inputs. Cross-module or real infra not needed to catch missing-field/rendering defects.
behaviors.test_description: Call confirmation formatter/template with representative visit types and assert rendered message contains required fields and correct preparation instructions.

behaviors.description: Pipeline orchestration (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes correctly with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets module orchestration with deterministic stubbed triage; this is cross-module state handoff without real model judgment. Agentic rejected by concrete text 'deterministic stubbed triage output'. System rejected because real external infra is not required to validate orchestration correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end intake in-process with stubbed triage and controllable insurance/scheduling adapters; assert step order, state propagation, and successful completion through confirmation.

behaviors.description: The full live intake pipeline (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This is full pipeline execution with live triage and real external dependencies; success depends on sequenced stage handoffs across the complete flow, including an agentic step. Lower levels rejected because they cannot expose end-to-end failures across validate→insurance→triage→schedule→confirm under live conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a real patient intake through production-like environment using live payer API integration and live triage model; assert unattended completion with persisted appointment and sent confirmation.
